info.title: EtStockX API
info.description:
EtStockX — Digital Stock Exchange and Brokerage Platform (Pre-Trade Coordination).

## Modules
- **Identity & Access (IAM)** — Registration, login, session management, MFA, broker verification
- **Profiles** — Client & broker profile management, investor activation, watchlists, public broker directory
- **Market Discovery** — Listings catalog, broker listing management, admin moderation; NLP search (English/Amharic) via GPT-4o-mini
- **Trade & Request Management** — Buy/sell requests, broker proposals, terms agreement, post-agreement order status, full order history
- **Messaging & Collaboration** — Secure broker–client chat with real-time delivery (SignalR `/hubs/chat`), attachments, read receipts, keyword search

## Roles
| Role | Description |
|------|-------------|
| Client | Basic access upon registration. Must complete profile to activate investor status for transactional actions. |
| Broker | ECMA-licensed intermediary (requires admin verification). |
| Dealer | Licensed principal trader (requires admin verification). |
| Admin  | Platform operator — verifies brokers, manages users, views audit logs. |

## Authentication
All protected endpoints require a JWT Bearer token in the `Authorization` header.
Access tokens expire after **15 minutes**. Use the refresh-token endpoint to obtain a new pair.

JWT claims: `sub` (userId), `role` (Client/Broker/Dealer/Admin), `isActivated` (bool).

**Backend README (EtStockX.Api):** IAM is a modular-monolith module; local defaults are HTTP `http://localhost:5163` and HTTPS `https://localhost:7039`, with Swagger UI at `/swagger`. Registration: **POST /api/v1/auth/register** (JSON, `role: Client`); broker/dealer onboarding with files: **POST /api/v1/auth/register/broker** (`multipart/form-data`). Investor capabilities are gated by profile activation (`isActivated`), not by substituting roles for Client.

post.summary: Register a new client account.
post.description:
Creates a new client account.
            
**Email verification:** A verification email is sent immediately after registration.
The user must click the link in the email to confirm their address before they can log in.
The verification link expires after **24 hours**. Use the resend-verification endpoint if needed.
            
**Client registration:**
- Account is immediately **Active** after registration with basic access (viewing only).
- `isActivated` starts as `false` — the client must complete their profile to activate investor status.
- Once activated, the client can perform transactional actions (buy/sell interest requests).
- Fields `licenseNumber`, `institution`, and `ecmaReference` should be omitted or null.
            
**Password rules:** minimum 8 characters, at least one uppercase, one lowercase, one digit, one special character.
            
### Example — Client
```json
{
  "role": "Client",
  "email": "abebe@example.com",
  "password": "Strong@123",
  "fullName": "Abebe Kebede",
  "phone": "+251911223344",
  "preferredLang": "am"
}
```
            
Broker and dealer onboarding is handled by the dedicated multipart endpoint:
`POST /api/v1/auth/register/broker`.

201.description: Client created successfully. A verification email has been sent.

400.description: Validation failed, the role is not Client, or the email already exists.

post.summary: Register a broker or dealer together with verification documents.
post.description:
Use this multipart endpoint for broker/dealer onboarding when the applicant must submit
license scans or other review files at registration time.
            
Upload one or more files in the `documents` form field. Optional `documentTypes` values may
be supplied in the same order to label each file for the admin review experience.

201.description: Broker/dealer created and documents uploaded successfully.

400.description: Validation failed, the email already exists, or uploaded files are invalid.

get.summary: Confirm a user's email address using the verification token sent during registration.
get.description:
The verification link is sent to the user's email upon registration. The token expires after 24 hours.
If the token has expired, use the resend-verification endpoint to get a new one.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: The verification token from the email link.

post.summary: Resend the email verification link.
post.description:
Use this if the original verification email was not received or the token has expired.
A new verification token will be generated and sent to the provided email address.
For security, this endpoint returns success even if the email does not exist.

requestBody.description: The email address to resend verification to.

200.description: Verification email sent (if the account exists and is not already confirmed).

post.summary: Authenticate and obtain JWT access + refresh tokens.
post.description:
Validates credentials and returns a token pair. The **access token** (15-min lifetime) is used
in the `Authorization: Bearer {token}` header for all protected endpoints.
            
**JWT claims included:**
- `sub` — user ID (GUID)
- `role` — `Client`, `Broker`, `Dealer`, or `Admin`
- `isActivated` — `true` / `false` (indicates whether the client has completed profile activation)
            
**Email verification:** the user must confirm their email address before logging in.
If email is not confirmed, a 401 response is returned with instructions to check their inbox.
            
**Account lockout:** after 5 consecutive failed attempts the account is locked for 15 minutes.
            
**MFA:** if the user has MFA enabled, the `otpCode` field is required. Use the 6-digit
time-based code from the authenticator app.
            
### Example
```json
{
  "email": "abebe@example.com",
  "password": "Strong@123",
  "otpCode": null
}
```

401.description: Invalid credentials, account locked, pending verification, or invalid MFA code.

post.summary: Exchange a refresh token for a new access + refresh token pair.
post.description:
Implements **refresh token rotation**: each use invalidates the old refresh token
and issues a brand-new pair. This limits the window of abuse if a refresh token is compromised.
            
The new access token will carry the **latest** `isActivated` claim value, so if the client
completed their profile since the last login the updated activation status is reflected immediately.
            
### Example
```json
{
  "refreshToken": "base64-encoded-token..."
}
```

401.description: Refresh token is invalid, expired, or already revoked.

post.summary: Change the authenticated user's password.
post.description:
The current password must be confirmed first. The new password must meet complexity rules:
minimum 8 characters, at least one uppercase, lowercase, digit, and special character.
            
### Example
```json
{
  "currentPassword": "OldPass@123",
  "newPassword": "NewSecure@456"
}
```

400.description: Current password incorrect or new password doesn't meet complexity rules.

post.summary: Enable MFA (two-factor authentication) for the current user.
post.description:
Generates a TOTP secret and enables MFA on the account.
The returned `secret` should be used to configure an authenticator app (e.g., Google Authenticator).
Future logins will require the `otpCode` field.
            
Password confirmation is required as a security measure.
            
### Example
```json
{
  "password": "MyPass@123"
}
```

400.description: Invalid password or MFA already enabled.

post.summary: Disable MFA (two-factor authentication) for the current user.
post.description:
Removes the TOTP secret and disables MFA. Future logins will no longer require an OTP code.
            
Password confirmation is required as a security measure.
            
### Example
```json
{
  "password": "MyPass@123"
}
```

400.description: Invalid password or MFA not enabled.

get.summary: List all active sessions for the current user.
get.description:
Returns all non-revoked, non-expired sessions including device info and IP address.
Users can use this to see where they are logged in and terminate unwanted sessions.

delete.description:
Immediately invalidates the session's refresh token. The associated access token will
continue to work until its natural expiry (max 15 minutes), but no new tokens can be
obtained from the revoked refresh token.
            
Users can only revoke their own sessions.

400.description: Session not found or belongs to another user.

get.summary: Get all pending broker/dealer verification applications.
get.description:
**Requires: Admin role.**
            
Returns a list of broker/dealer applications with status `Pending`.
Each application includes the applicant's details, license number, institution,
ECMA reference, and uploaded documents.

200.description: List of pending applications (may be empty).

get.summary: Download a broker verification document for admin review.
Get detailed information about a specific broker application.
get.description:
**Requires: Admin role.**
            
Returns full details including uploaded documents, review history, and decision reason (if any).

post.summary: Review a broker/dealer application — approve, reject, or request more information.
post.description:
**Requires: Admin role.**
            
| Decision   | Effect                                                                                    |
|------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `Approve`  | Application → `Verified`, user status → `Verified`, broker permissions granted.            |
| `Reject`   | Application → `Rejected`, user status → `Rejected`. `reason` field is **required**.       |
| `MoreInfo` | Application → `MoreInfoRequired`, user status → `MoreInfoRequired`. `reason` is required. |
            
All decisions are recorded in the audit log with the admin's user ID and timestamp.
            
### Example — Approve
```json
{
  "decision": "Approve",
  "reason": null
}
```
            
### Example — Reject
```json
{
  "decision": "Reject",
  "reason": "License document is expired. Please upload a current ECMA license."
}
```

parameters.name: applicationId
parameters.description: The broker application ID to review.

400.description: Invalid decision, missing reason, or application not found.

get.summary: Browse active listings, paginated, newest first. Optional filters.

post.summary: Broker publishes a new listing.

get.summary:
Natural-language search. Accepts English or Amharic. GPT-4o-mini extracts entities
(instrument, sector, price bounds); fallback to raw keyword search if NLP fails.
Listing detail — also records a View event and increments view_count.

put.summary: Broker updates one of their listings. Ownership is enforced by the handler.

patch.summary: Change a listing's lifecycle state: pause | resume | close.

get.summary: Broker's own listings (all statuses).
Analytics: views, inquiries, terms-agreed, conversion rate for a given listing.
Admin moderation queue — listings with status=PendingModeration.

post.summary: Admin moderates a listing: Approved | Rejected | Hidden | Clarification.

get.summary: Lists conversations for the current user with last-message preview and unread count.
Paginated message history for a conversation (newest first for "load more" scroll).
In-conversation keyword search.

post.summary: Sends a text message. Supply either ConversationId or RecipientId.
Uploads a document (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, JPEG, PNG, max 10 MB) as a new message in a conversation.
Marks unread messages in the conversation as read (used when opening a chat).

get.summary: Downloads a specific attachment. Access is restricted to conversation participants.
Get the authenticated broker's own profile.

put.description:
All fields are optional — only provided fields will be updated.
            
### Example
```json
{
  "institution": "Capital Securities PLC",
  "bio": "Licensed broker specializing in equities and bonds.",
  "licenseDisplay": "ECMA-BRK-001",
  "specializations": ["equities", "bonds"],
  "isAcceptingRequests": true
}
```

400.description: Validation error or profile not found.

get.summary: Browse verified broker profiles (public directory).
Get the authenticated client's own profile.

put.summary: Update the authenticated client's profile fields.
put.description:
All fields are optional — only provided fields will be updated.
This does **not** complete/activate the profile. Use `POST /profiles/client/complete` for that.
            
### Example
```json
{
  "address": "Bole, Addis Ababa",
  "contactPerson": "Abebe Kebede",
  "preferredLang": "am"
}
```

400.description: Validation error or profile not found.

post.summary: Complete the client profile to activate investor/transactional capabilities.
post.description:
All fields are **required**. Once completed, the system triggers investor activation
in the IAM module — the user's `isActivated` claim becomes `true` on next token refresh.
            
### Example
```json
{
  "riskProfile": "Moderate",
  "address": "Bole, Addis Ababa",
  "contactPerson": "Abebe Kebede",
  "settlementBank": "Commercial Bank of Ethiopia",
  "accountNickname": "My Trading Account"
}
```

400.description: Validation error, profile not found, or already completed.

get.summary: Get the authenticated client's watchlist.

200.description: Watchlist items ordered by display order.

post.summary: Add a stock listing to the client's watchlist.

400.description: Already in watchlist or invalid listing.

delete.summary: Remove a stock listing from the client's watchlist.

post.summary: Activated client initiates a buy request against a listing or directly with a broker.

get.summary: List the authenticated client's buy requests with optional filters.
Buy request detail with proposals and full order history.

patch.summary: Modify or cancel a buy request (only allowed before broker locks it).

post.summary: Activated client initiates a sell request to a broker.

get.summary: List the authenticated client's sell requests with optional filters.
Sell request detail with proposals and full order history.

patch.summary: Modify or cancel a sell request (only allowed before broker locks it).

post.summary: Client confirms (accept/reject) the broker's proposal.

get.summary: Broker's queue of incoming buy/sell requests, newest first.

post.summary: Broker sends a counter-proposal on a buy or sell request.

patch.summary: Broker advances post-agreement order status (ForwardedToESX, PartiallyFilled, Filled, Rejected).

get.summary: Broker views a specific client's trade history (only requests handled by this broker).
Broker dashboard: open-request counts grouped by lifecycle stage.

AddWatchlistItemRequest.description: Add a listing to the watchlist.

ChangeListingStatusRequest.description: Target state for a listing status transition: "pause" | "resume" | "close".

currentPassword.description: The user's current password for verification.

newPassword.description: The new password (must meet complexity rules).

CompleteClientProfileRequest.description: Complete client profile — all fields required for investor activation.

otpCode.description: 6-digit MFA code (required only if MFA is enabled).

ModifyBuyRequest.description: Either modifies fields or cancels (set Cancel=true to terminate the request).

email.description: The email address to resend verification to.

Bearer.description:
Enter the JWT access token obtained from the login endpoint.

Example: `eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIs...`
